target.title: Add QA evidence summary normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- Align the QA evidence summary schema with the latest RFC 0007 implementation plan: schema v2 emits `profile` with only `smoke-ci` / `release`, plus explicit `model_live`, `channel_live`, `provider_fixture` / `provider_auth`, `channel_driver`, and `runner` fields.
- Add a compact top-level taxonomy reference for the landed `taxonomy.yaml` snapshot from #91512 (`sourcePath`, `version`, `processVersion`, `snapshotDate`, and `sourceRef`) so release artifacts can join evidence rows to the taxonomy later without embedding or copying taxonomy contents.
- Embed the foundational `evidence` block in QA suite and Telegram live summaries without adding the scorecard gate; summary files continue to live under the command `--output-dir` or the default `.artifacts/qa-e2e/<lane>-<timestamp>` path.
- Normalize legacy profile env aliases into the current schema output so retained operator env values do not emit old profile names.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite-summary.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.summary-json.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.summary-json.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt "Context for versioning: this PR has not landed. The committed branch currently contains an intermediate unshipped schemaVersion 2 without taxonomy, but the requested final landed contract is schemaVersion 2 with the required top-level taxonomy reference. Please review compatibility against origin/main / shipped v1 behavior, not against the unlanded intermediate branch commit."`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt "Context: this local change removes the unnecessary product-specific channel-driver evidence-summary test/commentary. Please review the remaining generic schema test coverage and ignore the removed product-specific example unless it leaves a real coverage gap."`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: QA Lab outputs now include an RFC 0007-aligned evidence summary shape, and Telegram live QA writes the same top-level evidence schema as suite summaries.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on macOS, with focused unit/schema tests and extension typecheck.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the formatter check, focused Vitest files through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, and local autoreview with explicit schema-versioning context for the unlanded intermediate branch shape. After removing the stale product-specific channel-driver example, reran the same formatter, focused Vitest, extension typecheck, and local autoreview.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed for `evidence-summary`, suite summary JSON, suite summary rendering, and Telegram live runtime tests; `pnpm tsgo:extensions` exited successfully; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: QA suite evidence defaults deterministic mock-provider proof to `profile: "smoke-ci"`, `model_live: false`, `channel_live: false`, and `provider_fixture: "mock-openai"`. Telegram live evidence defaults to `profile: "release"`, `model_live: true`, `provider_auth: "live-frontier"`, `channel_live: true`, and `channel_driver: "native"`. The schema keeps `channel_driver` as opaque channel-driver metadata while `runner` remains the execution substrate. Each summary includes taxonomy join metadata for `taxonomy.yaml` version 1 / process version 3 / snapshot `2026-05-26` / source ref `origin/main@41eef4a7965`, without embedding taxonomy surfaces or categories.

What was not tested: No scorecard gate was added or run, no channel-driver SDK lane was implemented, and no live Telegram provider lane was executed for this scoped schema/output-shape change.
